scenes.text: Hello! I am Ms. Zara. Today, we find numbers! Numbers are all around us. Let us look together!
Look at this clock. Can you see the numbers? One, two, three... all the way to twelve!
See this door. It has a number. That is the house number. It helps us find the home!
Here is a bus! The number tells us where it goes. Bus number seven goes to school!
Look at this calendar. Every single day has a number. Today is the fifteenth!
At a shop, we see prices. This pencil costs five rupees. Can you see the number five?
Open a book. Every page has a number. Can you find page ten? Great!
A phone has numbers too! We press numbers to call our friends. Try it!
Long ago, people used marks to count. One mark for each cow. That was so smart!
Let us count these cows together. One, two, three, four, five. That is five cows! Well done!
In a game, we keep score. Team A has three. Team B has five. Who is winning?
Numbers help us count, compare, and find things. Numbers are everywhere. They are our friends!
Today we found numbers on clocks, doors, buses, shops, and books. That was great learning!
Amazing! Now let us practice. I know you can do it. Let us go!

quiz.question: A clock has numbers from 1 to 12. How many numbers are on a clock face?
quiz.explanation: A clock has exactly 12 numbers — from 1 to 12. Next time you see a clock, count them!

quiz.question: Ms. Zara counts cows: one, two, three, four, five. How many cows are there?
quiz.explanation: Ms. Zara counted 5 cows! When we count one by one, the last number we say is the total.

quiz.question: Team A has 3 points. Team B has 5 points. How many points in total?
quiz.explanation: 3 + 5 = 8 points in total! We use numbers to keep score in games.

quiz.question: A pencil costs 5 rupees. How many pencils can you buy with 15 rupees?
quiz.explanation: 15 ÷ 5 = 3 pencils! Numbers on price tags help us know how much things cost.

quiz.question: Today is the 15th on the calendar. What will be tomorrow's date?
quiz.explanation: After the 15th comes the 16th! Every day on the calendar has a number that goes one more.
